info.name: Internet Stability Tracker API v3.3
info.description:
Complete API collection for Internet Stability Tracker v3.3 — advanced network monitoring with AI insights, security auditing, real-time diagnostics, and multi-device support.

## Live Production URL
All requests default to the **live deployment**:
```
https://backend-cold-butterfly-9535.fly.dev/api
```

## Switch to local dev
Change the `base_url` collection variable to `http://localhost:8000/api`.

## Swagger UI
- **Production**: https://backend-cold-butterfly-9535.fly.dev/docs
- **Local**: http://localhost:8000/docs

## WebSocket
```
wss://backend-cold-butterfly-9535.fly.dev/api/ws/live
```

## Authentication
All per-device endpoints require `X-Client-ID` header (UUID). The frontend auto-generates and persists this in localStorage.

## Rate limits
| Endpoint | Limit |
|----------|-------|
| `POST /test-now` | 5 req / 60 s per IP + X-Client-ID |
| `POST /reports` | 5 req / 60 s per IP + X-Client-ID |
| `DELETE /measurements` | 3 req / 60 s (admin key required) |
| `GET /my-connection` | 10 req / 60 s per IP |
| All others | 60 req / 60 s per IP |

## New in v3.3
- ISP Contract Tracker — save plan details, track SLA compliance
- Network Quality Certificate — printable A+→F grade certificate
- Best Time Recommender — 24-hour speed profile with activity suggestions
- Multi-Device Aggregator — link devices via WiFi network code (no Bluetooth), QR + 6-char code
- DNS Monitor — per-resolver latency test
- ISP Complaint Letter Generator — auto-generate formal letter from your data
- Scheduled Speed Tests — recurring tests by hour/day with burst support
- Packet Loss & Jitter Monitor — TCP-based measurement with history
- WFH Score — work-from-home suitability across 8 apps
- Uptime Calendar — 90-day daily uptime heatmap
- ISP Community Status — aggregate ISP health across all users
- Speed Trend — multi-week improving/stable/declining trend

variable.description: Production URL. Change to http://localhost:8000/api for local dev.
Device UUID — update to match your browser's stored X-Client-ID
ID of a community report — update after creating one
ID of a registered webhook — update after calling POST /webhooks

request.description: Returns service status, UTC timestamp, and environment.
Triggers an immediate speed test. Rate limited: 5 req/60s.
Returns public IP, ISP, ASN, org, country, region, city, lat/lon, and last speed test.
Requires admin API key. Rate limited: 3 req/60s.
Returns gaming score (0-100), grade (A+ to F), per-game recommendations, ping/jitter/packet-loss.
Predicts HD/SD quality and max participants for Zoom, Teams, Google Meet.
Detects if router needs a reboot based on 24h performance trends. Requires 5+ tests.
What can I do? Returns recommended and not-recommended activities based on current speed/ping.
Checks external services (Google, Cloudflare, AWS) and compares with other ISP users.
Runs packet loss, jitter, bufferbloat, MTU, and DNS leak tests in parallel.
Checks if DNS resolver is a known secure provider (1.1.1.1, 8.8.8.8, 9.9.9.9, etc.).

query.description: Optional: specify VPN interface name (tun0, wg0, ppp0)

request.description: Runs port scan, privacy score, intrusion detection, and VPN recommendation in parallel.
Runs root cause analysis and returns answer + root_causes + recommendations.
Returns focused upload speed analysis: avg, peak, quality label, tip.

item.name: AI chatbot — compare upload vs download

request.description: Returns upload/download ratio, connection type (symmetric/download-heavy/upload-heavy), and peak values.

item.name: AI chatbot — best time to use internet

query.value: When is the best time to use my internet
Is my connection good for gaming

request.description: Returns A-D gaming grade, ping quality, and suitability verdict.
Greeting response — returns friendly welcome + live stats snapshot.
Returns top 3 best hours to download in the next N hours. Uses day-of-week weighted historical patterns.
Hourly congestion scores (0-100) for next 24 hours. Includes notable_periods with human-readable descriptions like 'Evening congestion (7 PM-11 PM) — 35% slower'.
Single endpoint returning all four predictions plus a headline summary. Includes data_quality: insufficient/limited/good.
Returns last N alert delivery records for this device (success/failure, channel, severity).
Returns all active webhooks registered for this device (max 5).
Register a URL to receive JSON payloads on outage, speed_drop, and recovery events. Max 5 webhooks per device.
Permanently removes a webhook. Only the owning device can delete it.
Sends a test payload to the webhook URL and returns HTTP status code.
Returns plain-text Prometheus metrics: total measurements, outages, reports, 24h avg download/upload/ping. Scrape with Grafana or any Prometheus-compatible stack.

query.description: ISP promised download speed in Mbps
ISP promised upload speed in Mbps

request.description: Compares actual measured speeds against ISP promised speeds. Returns compliance %, grade (A-F), and per-metric stats (avg/min/max/p10/p90). SLA met = 80% of promised speed threshold.

query.description: Optional known baseline speed. 0 = auto-detect from DB

request.description: Probes 4 CDN endpoints (Cloudflare, jsDelivr, Google CDN, Fastly) in parallel. Returns is_throttled, confidence, avg/min/max Mbps, and per-probe results. Ratio < 0.4 vs baseline = throttled.
Returns 0–100 score with letter grade (A+/A/B/C/D/F). Weighted: download 25%, upload 20%, ping 25%, stability 15%, uptime 15%. Includes breakdown per metric and actionable tips.
Natural-language weekly summary comparing last 7 days vs previous 7 days. Returns headline, narrative, week1/week2 stats, deltas, best/worst hour, outage count, and improvement tips.
Returns cost_per_plan_mbps, cost_per_actual_mbps, efficiency rating (good/average/poor), benchmark vs US avg ($0.47/Mbps) and global avg ($0.65/Mbps), and a verdict string.
Compares avg download/upload/ping between two date ranges. Returns deltas as % change and a verdict (Improvement/Declined/No change). Omit all params to compare last 30d vs previous 30d.
Finds the device's personal best download and upload speeds and upserts a leaderboard entry. Requires X-Client-ID. Max one entry per device.
Downloads a CSV file with all measurements. Response Content-Type: text/csv; filename: speed_data_{date}.csv. Use 'Send and Download' in Postman.
Downloads a JSON file with all measurements plus export metadata (device, exported_at, total_records). Use 'Send and Download' in Postman.
Returns all active API keys for this device (label, created_at, last_used, is_active). Raw key is never shown after creation.
Generates a new API key (UUID v4). The raw key is returned ONCE in the response — store it securely. Only the SHA-256 hash is stored in the database. Max 5 keys per device.
Permanently deactivates an API key. Replace KEY_ID_HERE with the numeric key ID from the list endpoint.
Returns a per-ISP report card with grades (A-F) for download, upload, ping, and uptime over the specified window. Sourced from community measurements.
Sends a test message to a Slack incoming webhook. Set platform to 'slack' or 'teams'. Returns http_status and success boolean.
Sends a test Adaptive Card message to a Microsoft Teams incoming webhook.

Returns a day-by-day uptime % grid for GitHub-style heatmap visualization. Each day has: date, uptime_pct, total tests, outage count, level (0–3). Also returns summary: avg_uptime_pct, perfect_days, outage_days, tracked_days.

query.description: ISP name to query. Omit to auto-detect from last measurement.

request.description: Aggregates anonymous speed data from all users with the same ISP. Returns status (healthy/degraded/outage), outage_pct, avg_download, unique_devices, and a verdict string. If isp_name is omitted, uses your last recorded ISP.

Detects if speed is improving, stable, or declining over multiple weeks. Returns trend (improving/stable/declining/insufficient_data), trend_pct (% change first→last week), a verdict string, and per-week breakdown of avg download/upload/ping.
Real-time speed measurements over WebSocket. Use Postman's WebSocket client or wscat.

item.name: v3.3 — New Features
item.description: ISP Contract, Certificate, Best Time, Multi-Device, DNS Monitor, Complaint Letter, Schedules, Packet Loss, WFH Score

request.description: Returns my_ip (shared public IP for all WiFi devices), nearby list, and already_linked status. Use my_ip to derive the deterministic WiFi group code client-side.
Leave group_id null to create a new group. Pass a group_id to join an existing group.
